channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Black Hat USA
item.description: No summary available
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: FBI warns against using Chinese mobile apps due to privacy risks
item.description: The U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) warned Americans against using foreign-developed mobile applications, particularly those created by Chinese developers. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: 3 Reasons Attackers Are Using Your Trusted Tools Against You (And Why You Don’t See It Coming)
item.description: For years, cybersecurity has followed a familiar model: block malware, stop the attack. Now, attackers are moving on to what’s next. Threat actors now use malware less frequently in favor of what’s al...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Are We Training AI Too Late?
item.description: Ask the Expert: Cybersecurity teams need to expand their field of view to include new, unique threat sources, rather than relying on past, proven threat actors....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Google fixes fourth Chrome zero-day exploited in attacks in 2026
item.description: Google has fixed the fourth Chrome vulnerability exploited in zero-day attacks since the start of the year. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Google Attributes Axios npm Supply Chain Attack to North Korean Group UNC1069
item.description: Google has formally attributed the supply chain compromise of the popular Axios npm package to a financially motivated North Korean threat activity cluster tracked as UNC1069. "We have attributed the ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Google Drive ransomware detection now on by default for paying users
item.description: Google announced that the AI-powered Google Drive ransomware detection feature has reached general availability and is now enabled by default for all paying users. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Claude Code Source Leaked via npm Packaging Error, Anthropic Confirms
item.description: Anthropic on Tuesday confirmed that internal code for its popular artificial intelligence (AI) coding assistant, Claude Code, had been inadvertently released due to a human error. "No sensitive custom...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New Windows 11 emergency update fixes preview update install issues
item.description: Microsoft released an emergency update to fix the March 2026 KB5079391 non-security preview update, which was pulled over the weekend due to installation issues. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Claude Code source code accidentally leaked in NPM package
item.description: Anthropic says it accidentally leaked the source code for Claude Code, which is closed source, but the company says no customer data or credentials were exposed. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Google now allows you to change your @gmail.com address
item.description: Google is rolling out a new feature in the U.S. that allows users to change their @gmail address or create a new alias. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Proton launches new "Meet" privacy-focused conferencing platform
item.description: Proton has announced a new video conferencing service named Meet and positioned it as a privacy-focused alternative to mainstream services like Google Meet, Zoom, and Microsoft Teams. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: GIGABYTE Control Center vulnerable to arbitrary file write flaw
item.description: The GIGABYTE Control Center is vulnerable to an arbitrary file-write flaw that could allow a remote, unauthenticated attacker to access files on vulnerable hosts. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: The Forgotten Endpoint: Security Risks of Dormant Devices
item.description: Technology Talk: That forgotten notebook holds plenty of secrets to enterprise access....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Claude AI finds Vim, Emacs RCE bugs that trigger on file open
item.description: Vulnerabilities in the Vim and GNU Emacs text editors, discovered using simple prompts with the Claude assistant, allow remote code execution simply by opening a file. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Axios NPM Package Compromised in Precision Attack
item.description: The NPM package for Axios, a popular JavaScript HTTP client library, was briefly compromised this week, possibly by North Korean threat actors....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Google's Vertex AI Has an Over-Privileged Problem
item.description: Palo Alto researchers show how attackers could exploit AI agents on Google's Vertex AI to steal data and break into restricted cloud infrastructure....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: TeamPCP Breaches Cloud, SaaS Instances With Stolen Credentials
item.description: The threat group's shift to speedy attacks on AWS, Azure, and SaaS instances shows organizations need to respond quickly to compromised credentials....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Android Developer Verification Rollout Begins Ahead of September Enforcement
item.description: Google on Monday said it's officially rolling out Android developer verification to all developers to combat the problem of bad actors distributing harmful apps while "hiding behind anonymity." The de...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Cisco source code stolen in Trivy-linked dev environment breach
item.description: Cisco has suffered a cyberattack after threat actors used stolen credentials from the recent Trivy supply chain attack to breach its internal development environment and steal source code belonging to...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: TrueConf Zero-Day Exploited in Attacks on Southeast Asian Government Networks
item.description: A high-severity security flaw in the TrueConf client video conferencing software has been exploited in the wild as a zero-day as part of a campaign targeting government entities in Southeast Asia dubb...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Rethinking Vulnerability Management Strategies for Mid-Market Security
item.description: Intruder's Chris Wallis argues mid-market teams should prioritize CVE remediation speed over vulnerability counts, while expanding defenses beyond CVEs to include attack surface management....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: AI and Quantum Are Forcing a Rethink of Digital Trust
item.description: In a conversation with Dark Reading’s Terry Sweeney, DigiCert CEO Amit Sinha explains how AI-driven identities and quantum threats are reshaping the foundations of digital trust....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: How to Categorize AI Agents and Prioritize Risk
item.description: AI agent risk isn't equal, it scales with access to systems and level of autonomy. Token Security explains how CISOs should categorize agents and prioritize what to secure first. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Hackers compromise Axios npm package to drop cross-platform malware
item.description: Hackers hijacked the npm account of the Axios package, a JavaScript HTTP client with 100M+ weekly downloads, to deliver remote access trojans to Linux, Windows, and macOS systems. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Iran Deploys 'Pseudo-Ransomware,' Revives Pay2Key Operations
item.description: Iranian APTs are blurring the lines between state-sponsored and cybercriminal activities to target high-impact US organizations....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Vertex AI Vulnerability Exposes Google Cloud Data and Private Artifacts
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a security "blind spot" in Google Cloud's Vertex AI platform that could allow artificial intelligence (AI) agents to be weaponized by an attacker to gain unaut...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft fixes Outlook Classic crashes caused by Teams Meeting add-in
item.description: ​Microsoft has resolved a known issue that rendered the classic Outlook email client unusable for users who enabled the Microsoft Teams Meeting Add-in. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: The AI Arms Race – Why Unified Exposure Management Is Becoming a Boardroom Priority
item.description: The cybersecurity landscape is accelerating at an unprecedented rate. What is emerging is not simply a rise in the number of vulnerabilities or tools, but a dramatic increase in speed. Speed of attack...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Silver Fox Expands Asia Cyber Campaign with AtlasCross RAT and Fake Domains
item.description: Chinese-speaking users are the target of an active campaign that uses typosquatted domains impersonating trusted software brands to deliver a previously undocumented remote access trojan named AtlasCr...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News
